# Echo Persistence — Overview

> The course hub and the one idea behind all fourteen modules: EchoMQ enqueues on Valkey (a job on disk within a 1s window, on one box); Echo Persistence is everything beyond that for state that must not be lost or must live off the machine. Champ accepts at in-heap speed with an fsync per K records, a transactional page-store commits each batch as one LSN replicated to Tigris, and the same model is built twice — native Elixir on CubDB and Rust on Fjall. The overview establishes that one idea in three dives, then sends you into Module 1.

This section is the pre-chapter overview. It has no sub-modules: a hub and three dives, read in the order below. Links are to the readable `.md` sources (each has a rendered `.html` sibling).

## Navigation

- **Up: course root** — [../llms.txt](../llms.txt) · the course landing/map — [../index.md](../index.md)
- **Overview hub** — the one knob (durability and speed are the same dial), the four-way shootout, and the two-tier answer (Champ + Graft) — [index.md](index.md)

## The three dives

- **Dive 1 · The durability spectrum** — the four-way shootout read mechanically, and the two independent axes: the loss window, and whether state leaves the box — [the-durability-spectrum.md](the-durability-spectrum.md)
- **Dive 2 · The two-tier shape** — Champ accepts at in-heap speed; Graft commits one LSN, replicated to Tigris behind a conditional-write fence. The seam, and the per-call durability mode — [the-two-tier-shape.md](the-two-tier-shape.md)
- **Dive 3 · Persistence in the platform** — the four storage tiers, Echo Bus beside Echo Persistence, the durability dial per queue, and the two engines that coexist — [persistence-in-the-platform.md](persistence-in-the-platform.md)

## Next

Continue to **Chapter I · Foundations** — [../foundations/llms.txt](../foundations/llms.txt)
